metrics.narrative: No quantified ROI or cost-savings case has been articulated by FGV in any recorded exchange. The RFP scoping response (11 Feb 2026, Mohd Sharil Mohd Sharil Abdul Hamid) focuses purely on operational volumetrics - 60,000-62,000 RFx events/year, 1,500 users, 10,000-12,000 suppliers, ~24 business units - rather than a business case for switching off SAP ECC. The qualitative pain is procurement complexity across a large multi-entity conglomerate, but no cost, cycle-time, or error-rate figures have been quoted by any FGV stakeholder. Quantifying projected savings/ROI for a senior FGV audience is the move to close this gap.

sources: Task 23 Feb 2026 (Norzaida Jamal, Mohd Sharil Mohd Sharil Abdul Hamid)
champion.narrative: No internal FGV champion is currently evident. The strongest current relationship is transactional: Mac Chew engages cordially with the relevant stakeholder (declining a Horizon event invite politely on 15 Jun 2026), and Norzaida Jamal and Mohd Sharil Mohd Sharil Abdul Hamid ran the RFP exchange professionally in Feb 2026 - but none has been shown advocating internally for Zycus, sharing internal documents beyond requested RFP responses, or pushing back on SAP on Zycus's behalf. The deal instead runs through KPMG as the external partner channel ('KPMG well placed here,' 16 Aug 2024 Next Step), which is an access channel, not an internal champion.

competition.narrative: SAP is the clearest and most current threat: FGV runs SAP ECC as its ERP of record and vendor master (scoping email, 11 Feb 2026), and the Next Step log flagged SAP as 'our main competitor in this account' as far back as 30 May 2024. During the FGV-FELDA merger, SAP is well placed to bundle Ariba into a broader ERP renewal at a discount, trading on existing integration and perceived lower risk. Coupa, GEP Worldwide, and iValua remain logged on Competitors__c but carry no fresh call or email evidence this sweep confirming active buyer evaluation; they are carried forward at lower current confidence. Zycus's wedge is the multi-entity (24 business unit), high-volume (60k+ RFx events/year) S2P depth a bolt-on ERP module may not cleanly cover, but this has not yet been put to the buyer explicitly.

identify_pain.narrative: FGV's stated driver is a broader 'transformation and digital enablement agenda' and modernizing an S2P environment currently run on SAP ECC plus manual/email-based collaboration (RFP invitation, 29 Jan 2026; scoping response, 11 Feb 2026). A distinct organizational pain, internal cross-department alignment friction, was raised by Siti Balquish Sanudin (Head, Supplier Contract & Relationship Management) on 21 Mar 2025, when she told Zycus's rep the project was on hold because FGV was 'having difficulty getting other users and departments to give the green light for this solution.' Neither the operational nor the internal-alignment pain has been quantified financially.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, legal, or InfoSec activity has started; the deal has not passed the RFP-shortlist gate. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank, so there is no forcing-function date from an existing FGV contract. KPMG is named in the Next Step history as FGV's 'preferred partner' (19 Jun 2024 entry) and Zycus has coordinated with KPMG rather than negotiating paper directly, but no contracting mechanics have surfaced given the pre-shortlist stage.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer with confirmed budget authority has been named across three years of exchanges. Mac Chew (Head of Group Procurement, Decision Maker per Contact Role) is the senior-most named contact and the person Zycus's VP APAC engages directly, but no CFO, CPO, or board-level sponsor is on record. The 1 Jun 2026 Next Step explicitly frames connecting a senior FGV exec with the deal owner's manager as still outstanding, meaning even Zycus leadership has not yet reached a senior FGV sponsor. The FGV-FELDA merger and senior-team restructuring raise real risk that budget authority is unsettled or about to change hands.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a structured RFP/market-study run by FGV's e-procurement team: Norzaida Jamal issued the RFP on 29 Jan 2026, Zycus submitted on 23 Feb 2026, and the outcome feeds an RFP shortlist decision FGV said would land 'after Hari Raya' (mid-April 2026) but has since slipped, per the 1 Jun 2026 Next Step, to 'third quarter' visibility, attributed to the FGV-FELDA merger and a senior-team restructuring. No post-shortlist steps (technical deep-dive, commercial negotiation, contracting) have been confirmed or scheduled.

sources: Task 11 Feb 2026 (Mohd Sharil Mohd Sharil Abdul Hamid)
decision_criteria.narrative: FGV ran a formal RFP ('FGV S2P System - Market Study & Opportunity Assessment') with a compliance checklist and pricing template, issued 29 Jan 2026 by Norzaida Jamal and answered by Zycus on 23 Feb 2026. The scoping exchange (11 Feb 2026, Mohd Sharil Mohd Sharil Abdul Hamid) shows criteria spanning Sourcing, Contracting, Supplier Management, and P2P coverage across ~24 business units, integration with the existing SAP ECC ERP and vendor master, and multi-entity role/data segregation. FGV explicitly said GenAI capability 'is not positioned as a mandatory requirement,' directly undercutting the Merlin AI component of the deal. FGV itself called this a 'market study,' not a final technical spec, so criteria are documented at a high, indicative level rather than a locked scorecard.

north_star_verdict.math: 136 days to close (07 Jul 2026 -> 20 Nov 2026). Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c = 26 Feb 2026, so the deal has sat in Formal Evaluation for ~131 days with Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c still blank. Close date was pushed twice in 2026: 20 Jun -> 20 Sep (03 May 2026) and 20 Sep -> 20 Nov (11 May 2026). With shortlist visibility now Q3 2026 (per 1 Jun 2026 Next Step) and no shortlist yet issued, the remaining ~2-4 months would need to cover shortlisting, technical validation, commercial negotiation, and contracting for a $400,000, 7-module deployment - an aggressive compression.
evidence: Next_Step_History__c (1 Jun 2026): shortlist visibility pushed from April to Q3 2026 due to the FGV-FELDA merger and senior-team restructuring.

north_star_verdict.headline: RFP submitted and buyer engagement continues, but the shortlist decision the buyer itself promised for April 2026 has slipped a full quarter to Q3 2026, and the exec-connect planned for mid-June 2026 has not been confirmed as having happened.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline category is already appropriately conservative, but the 20 Nov 2026 close date is not realistic given no shortlist has been issued; recommend re-dating to Q1 2027 pending the Q3 2026 shortlist outcome.

flags: Reframe as merger-critical unification platform: the FGV-FELDA merger creates a forcing function (duplicate procurement systems, fragmented supplier bases, contract chaos) that Zycus can solve; position the platform as the post-merger rationalization layer and quantify the savings (supplier consolidation, contract compliance, spend visibility) to elevate this from a discretionary RFP to a strategic imperative.

Map and engage the Economic Buyer at CFO or CPO level: a $400k full-suite deal this old needs C-level budget ownership; use KPMG's relationship and the deal owner's manager (Carl Kimball) to secure an executive connection and convert the EB from passive approver to active sponsor who will prioritize the shortlist decision and protect the budget through the merger.

Preempt SAP's ERP-bundling advantage with a TCO counterstrike: SAP will pitch Ariba as 'already integrated, lower risk, bundled discount' during the merger; get ahead by quantifying Zycus's best-of-breed TCO (faster ROI, no ERP lock-in, richer contract/AI features, KPMG delivery speed) and socializing it with Mac Chew and the EB before the shortlist closes to reframe SAP as the expensive, feature-shallow path.
